drift_arrival.text: They found shelter in a collapsed overpass — a bridge that once spanned a river long since dried.
Rain began to fall. Not water. Ash-colored drops that left gray streaks on skin.

drift_arrival.speaker: Tobias
drift_arrival.text: Memory rain. Residue from a combustion event somewhere upwind. Harmless, but unpleasant.

drift_arrival.speaker: Elia
drift_arrival.text: We should rest here. Arrel, you've been walking unevenly for the last hour.

drift_arrival.speaker: Elia
drift_arrival.text: You're not fine. You forgot the name of the waystation ten minutes after we left it.

reading_deterioration.text: Arrel opened the Blank Book. The letters on the first page swam and blurred.
Tobias took the book, examined it, then handed it back.

reading_deterioration.speaker: Tobias
reading_deterioration.text: The words aren't moving. Your ability to read them is... deteriorating.

reading_deterioration.speaker: Tobias
reading_deterioration.text: It's a known side effect of high-efficiency combustion. The brain prioritizes combat processing over literacy functions.

reading_deterioration.speaker: Elia
reading_deterioration.text: He's losing the ability to read?

reading_deterioration.speaker: Tobias
reading_deterioration.text: Temporarily. Probably. The Bureau documentation says 'temporary' but I've seen the footnotes.

anchoring_session.speaker: Elia
anchoring_session.text: Arrel. Sit down. Give me your hands.
She took his hands, palms up. Her fingers were warm against his wrists.

anchoring_session.speaker: Elia
anchoring_session.text: Close your eyes. Tell me something you remember. Not a big memory — something small.

anchoring_session.speaker: Arrel
anchoring_session.text: ...I remember the smell of bread. From a shop I passed once. The door was open and the heat came out like a wall.

anchoring_session.speaker: Elia
anchoring_session.text: Good. Hold that. Feel the heat.
Something shifted. Like a loose page being pressed back into its binding.

anchoring_session.speaker: Elia
anchoring_session.text: That's an anchor point. A memory too small to burn, too real to forget. I'm tying it to the architecture.

anchoring_session.speaker: Tobias
anchoring_session.text: Remarkable. I've read about anchoring but never seen it performed. May I take notes?
The world steadied. The letters in the Blank Book stopped swimming. For now.

tobias_explains_classification.speaker: Tobias
tobias_explains_classification.text: Since we have time — let me explain the classification system.

tobias_explains_classification.speaker: Tobias
tobias_explains_classification.text: The Bureau ranks Burners from Class One to Class Seven. Most never exceed Class Three.

tobias_explains_classification.speaker: Tobias
tobias_explains_classification.text: Class One: involuntary micro-burns. Forgetting where you put your keys. Everyone does it.

tobias_explains_classification.speaker: Tobias
tobias_explains_classification.text: Class Three: directed combustion. You choose what to burn. Most licensed Burners operate here.

tobias_explains_classification.speaker: Tobias
tobias_explains_classification.text: Class Five: cascade capability. You can burn multiple memories in sequence. The power scales exponentially.

tobias_explains_classification.speaker: Tobias
tobias_explains_classification.text: Class Seven: total architecture access. You can burn memories you don't even know you have. The substructure opens.

tobias_explains_classification.speaker: Arrel
tobias_explains_classification.text: And what happens to a Class Seven?

tobias_explains_classification.speaker: Tobias
tobias_explains_classification.text: Historically? They burn everything. Then they forget how to breathe.

tobias_explains_classification.speaker: Tobias
tobias_explains_classification.text: But that's just statistics. You have something they didn't.

tobias_explains_classification.speaker: Tobias
tobias_explains_classification.text: An anchor who doesn't charge by the hour.

tobias_explains_classification.speaker: Elia
tobias_explains_classification.text: I said don't call me that.

night_conversation.text: Night fell. Tobias snored softly by the fire. Elia sat with her back against the overpass wall.

night_conversation.speaker: Arrel
night_conversation.text: How long can you keep doing this? The anchoring.

night_conversation.speaker: Elia
night_conversation.text: As long as you need me to.

night_conversation.speaker: Elia
night_conversation.text: It's the only one I have.
They sat in silence for a while. The ash-rain had stopped, but the sky stayed gray.

night_conversation.speaker: Arrel
night_conversation.text: I'm starting to forget things I didn't burn. Small things. Names of streets. Faces.

night_conversation.speaker: Elia
night_conversation.text: I've been counting. You've lost eleven micro-memories in the last two days. Involuntary burns.

night_conversation.speaker: Elia
night_conversation.text: The architecture is destabilizing. Each intentional burn makes the walls thinner.
Arrel didn't answer. He wasn't sure he could.

drift_departure.text: Morning. The ash-rain left a thin gray layer on everything. Tobias was already writing in his journal.

drift_departure.speaker: Tobias
drift_departure.text: The coast is close. I can smell salt under the ash.

drift_departure.speaker: Arrel
drift_departure.text: Better. The letters are holding still.

drift_departure.speaker: Tobias
drift_departure.text: There's something you should know about the coast. The Bureau declared it unstable six months ago.

drift_departure.speaker: Tobias
drift_departure.text: The Voidline is eating the cliffs. Whole sections drop into the sea without warning.

drift_departure.speaker: Tobias
drift_departure.text: Not if you want to reach The Seam before whoever is following us does.
They packed up and moved north. The air grew colder. Salt began to creep into the ash.

arrel_flashback_home.text: ...the table was always set for three.
Warmth. The smell of bread. A voice calling your name.
The image fractures. You can't remember whose voice that was.
